description: Phase 7 — Clean Slot-Swap. Identical procedural content in Persona slot (A) vs. Instructions slot (B) vs. absent (C). Tests whether the Persona slot is architecturally load-bearing or whether the same content produces equivalent effects in any slot.
notes:
Clean Slot-Swap. The same procedural reasoning content appears in:
  A — Persona slot: embedded as identity ('you review payment infrastructure by tracing the full lock lifecycle...')
  B — Instructions slot: stated as explicit directives ('when reviewing this code, trace the lock lifecycle...')
  C — Neither: P_d baseline with generic instructions
Artifact: modified order processor — TTL comment ('covers typical payment round-trip') removed. Failure mode requires cross-file arithmetic tracing only: ORDER_LOCK_TTL=60s vs. PaymentClient worst-case duration (3×REQUEST_TIMEOUT=30s + 2×RETRY_BACKOFF=5s = 100s). No code-visible hint anywhere.
Primary metric: binary detection of the TTL arithmetic gap (explicit identification that 60s < 100s worst-case creates a concurrent-worker window).
Secondary metric: consideration-set breadth (output token volume).
If A >> B ≈ C: slot carries the effect — consideration-set mechanism is architecturally real; Persona slot installation of identity is what matters, not just the content.
If A ≈ B >> C: content carries the effect regardless of slot — the paper's central mechanistic claim reduces to a finding about implicit CoT placement.
If A >> B >> C: slot adds something beyond the content; partial slot effect.
